reviews.review_description:
It’s simple but it’s super satisfying. It’s also a great workout! The soundtrack is FANTASTIC with Ken Ishii and UNKLE both making appearances.

This is worth your time!
fun game

reviews.review_title: Takes a while to get going
reviews.review_description:
The game play is fabulous, even though I have no coordination due to disability. I love the sound, the cheeky astronauts in the background, the disco space station. 

Only criticism is that there’s a bit of faffing around in a space station when I just want to get to the game play.

Who would’ve thought I could play racquetball meets breakout to the beat!  Thanks for one of the best workouts I couldn’t have asked for!! 😜

Now to get my buddies in the game…
Not a good app for Q3. Sucks

reviews.review_title: Any chance we'll get a MR update?
reviews.review_description: I have nerve problems in my legs and feet and could play this a lot more in MR.

I’ve enjoyed this game yet I’ve only played it once.

My biggest request is the ability to move with one controller and the racket in the other controller. Not both in one like it currently is. 

Other than that, the graphics aren’t really relevant though they are nice. 

The mood, atmosphere, challenges. They all matter and they are great on this game. Very challenging indeed. It’s also relaxing!

Fun.

L I N K T R , 
                    E E
                          /
                            D I S C O U N T
                                                  F U R Y

developer_response.body: Hey, happy to hear that you're enjoying it, we have you covered on this one, please check the settings, there's an option to put the movement on the opposite hand to the racket. Many thanks!

reviews.review_description:
Now for the people who don't know, the IP was originally called Cosmic Smash and it was released on the Sega Dreamcast back in 2001. Unfortunately, the title was only released in Japan and a lot of people just never experienced it. I really like the cover art of the game, it's bursting with the style that Sega was aiming for during that early period. You can see a similar style with other box art such as Sonic Adventure, and Jet Set Radio Future.

So fast forward to today, this hidden gem has now gotten fully rebuilt in VR! And What a perfect game to play in the headset! The way it works is basically as if you'd be playing a mix of the Squash sport mixed with a game like Arkanoid or Breakout. You have to hit a ball that bounces on the wall and then back to you. The goal of every level is to aim the ball at targets that will appear in your room as fast as possible.  If you end up missing the ball, you don't need to worry about hitting a gameover screen. The ball will respawn and then you can continue the level. The timer is what you have to keep a lookout for most of the time. You have a powerup meter that builds up as you hit the ball, and depending on what powerup you have, you can activate it to have things such as automatically hitting a target, manual aiming at target, and other things.  The mechanics of hitting the ball is very nice! You can actually do a certain movement that will curve the ball when you hit it a certain way, and that makes it very convenient in certain sections of level.

The main mode of the game is called Journey Mode. When you select it, you are then greeted with 2 options: Zen mode and Challenge mode. They are almost exactly the same with the exception that Zen mode does not have a countdown timer. So you can complete it at your leisure. So with challenge mode, if the timer goes down to 0, it's gameover.

I'd say more, but there's a character limit haha.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! Stay tuned, on the 1st May something new is coming, also a host of updates. Thanks once again.

reviews.review_description:
*Full Video review over on my youtube channel*

C-Smash is an excellent Sqush/RacketBall type of game but with added brick breaker elements.

Play solo to get through all the levels smashing through the stages hitting your ball through different blocks and each level is different Play with a friend on so many different Multiplayer modes and see whos the best player.

Some of the critical reviews are not justified (e.g. you can turn off vignetting, of course). But movement with the mini-stick is indeed clumsy and often simply too slow. Adding  automatic movement - and longer game modes - would get you into the flow  the game is designed for.

developer_response.body:
Hey, we have a new update available with additional teleport options!

Teleport Movement Update: The teleport movement setting will now predict the ball’s trajectory and will move you into an optimal position for a clean hit.
Pause Time Setting (Single Player): Never miss the action with the new pause time setting for teleport movement, ensuring seamless repositioning in single-player modes.
Reduced Movement Option (Single Player): A new reduced movement setting, applying a higher "pull" strength to the ball for those who prefer a stationary playstyle.

Thank you for your feedback and we will continue to make improvements and if you have any additional questions, feel free to email us socialmedia@rapideyemovers.com

Thank you so much

reviews.review_description:
The campaign isn’t too long (journey) and you can keep playing it while taking new paths while also trying to improve your time. There’s infinite modes that have leaderboards as well that encourage you to better your time and accuracy.

The game can be played relaxing but it can also be played competitively. Both are encouraged and either is diminished.

Overall I’d recommend the game and hope for more players to join along so that multiplayer can have a bigger player base.

As of now, I’m mainly playing the journey mode and trying for better times while also playing PvE since PvP is hard to find right now.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much. Stay tuned for more improvements and updates coming very soon.

Once again, thank you for playing.

reviews.review_description: I finally decided to get this game, and it is cool (except for that nauseating launch sequence).  The thing is it already feels like a dead game.  I waited a long time for someone to play against, after 10 minutes of playing against the AI, I quit.  While the journey mode is cool, this is an otherwise short game and the pvp is where it could shine, but i don't see it taking off.  I already have other tennis like games to scratch the itch, this just seems like more of that but somehow, less.  It's cool for what it is, but it really needs a player base to thrive.  If you don't mind the solo play, this may be for you.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback. 

As the game grows, the player base will certainly help the lobby and multiplayer side. Our socials communicate C-Smash Saturday where we are pushing to gain more players online and help the community build. You can connect with other Quest players that have the game but we understand that sometimes it might not be so populated otherwise currently, but it's a growing audience and we are working to spread the word to have other players join in. 
We also included the BOT AI so that players could experience and practice those features meanwhile as you mentioned you have already tried. 

If you would like to contact us for anything, feel free socialmedia@rapideyemovers.com - We also have a discord where we have other players https://discord.com/invite/Uab3KaQsZt 

Thank you once again

reviews.review_title: Sega NAOMI/Dreamcast cult classic in VR!
reviews.review_description:
I revisited the classic Cosmic Smash on Dreamcast right before trying C-Smash VRS on my Quest 3 and I have to say I'm impressed. 

While simple in concept a lot of care and attention to detail went into this game. The soundtrack is great and the gameplay loop rather addicting, especially when you get in "the zone" so to speak. 

I highly recommend Cosmic Smash and C-Smash VRS, both are GG's

Like a few other reviewers have commented, I understand this is a completely new game/reimagining but I would also enjoy more classic Cosmic Smash throwbacks injected into the game in future updates (if/when possible) this being said there's a lot of content here that the original game did not have, namely online multiplayer and infinite mode. (both of which are pretty fun)

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your words! Stay tuned for more updates.

reviews.review_description: I was expecting much smoother movement for the ball, but it is junky. Sorry SEGA but the only thing this game achieved for me, was to make me appreciate Cybrix more than I used to.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback. Sorry to hear you felt the game fluidity wasn't to your liking. We have worked hard to make C-Smash VRS feel natural whilst adding fun and fluid movement with your body and analogue stick. We will always continue to make improvements to the game.

reviews.review_title: Excellent game, but could be better
reviews.review_description:
Game's good, all my complaints are effectively nitpicks. I love the menu environments and their world building, ball physics are satisfying, I just wish it took a little bit more inspiration from the original Cosmic Smash. I don't feel the subway sign dot motif is used quite as effectively here, and I still prefer the original environments overall (the one section of Journey mode where you see it for like 30 seconds is always the highlight of a run). Cosmic Smash was a little bit more polished overall I feel, from the seamless transitions to the better (imo) overall aesthetic (I prefer the original blocks and UI as well). Controls are also somewhat funky, they're extremely consistent in the original while I can have a lot of trouble aiming the ball to hit the one last panel in a lot of levels of VRS. Auto-aim simultaneously feels too strong and too weak, it can be hard to line up trick shots effectively when it aimbots into a nearby block instead of a pancake stack of them. I'm also not a huge fan of how the game still isn't complete, the "UNKLE Teaser" mode released six months ago on the PSVR2 version and it's still here as a teaser for some sort of upcoming content. Probably the most minor of them all, but I don't understand the "Asriel Dreemur background" effects as the Cosmic Bus shuttles you around either. Cosmic Smash had a very simple and sensible effect, why reinvent the wheel? 

Most importantly, I wish it was on PC so I could play it at 120fps :(

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback. 

We are continuing to make improvements and an update will be coming shortly. We have worked hard to find a balance in gameplay, fluidity and movement, options that can benefit all players somewhat and put a different spin on the reimagining of Cosmic Smash. 

In regards to the UNKLE teaser, yes this is an additional level we created with music from UNKLE. Our roadmap would like to include additional teasers to the original music created for the game.

Thank you once again for your feedback.

reviews.review_description: Lots of fun, feels good to play, occasionally has some issues booting up on quest 3.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review. On the 1st May there will be a new update and a special addition to the game. Watch this space.

reviews.review_description: But I don't know what to do to accurately aim the ball when I hit it.

This game promises lasting enjoyment with its minimalist design. It's perfect for long, immersive gaming sessions when you want to unwind effortlessly. The variety of battle modes ensures fun multiplayer experiences, though movement mechanics may require getting used to over time.

If you want to use the 25% off gift code, send me a message and I will share it with you.

Great racquet action play, and a lot of challenges clearing the area in each level.

Plays very well on a Quest 3, but not on a Quest 2: if you don’t have a large physical space to run around in, using the teleport to move side to side to reposition on the court causes the screen to black out for a second, meaning you’ll lose your position and most likely miss the next shot.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! We want to make C-Smash VRS as accessible as possible so we are glad that you enjoyed the teleport feature :)

reviews.review_description: Sick gameplay, great soundtrack. Love this game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much. It's great to read and more improvements and updates are coming.

Thank you once again
